title: Carolina Renewable Products Breaks Ground on $280 Million Renewable Diesel Facility in Orangeburg County
content: Carolina Renewable Products (CRP) has officially broken ground on its first renewable diesel facility in Orangeburg County, marking the launch of a $280 million clean-energy investment that will create 155 jobs over the next five years. Located at a 20-acre site at 546 Prosperity Drive, the plant will convert pine wood residuals from local sawmills into renewable dieselâ€”a clean, drop-in fuel. The facility will also produce high-value by-products, including biochar and wood vinegar. Construction begins in spring 2026, with initial modules operating by late 2026 and full operations expected by 2027. The project is supported by county tax incentives, a $755,000 Closing Fund grant, and a $200,000 grant from South Carolina's Secretary of Agriculture. State leaders praised the project for strengthening South Carolinaâ€™s forestry sector, offering a new market for wood residuals amidst recent mill closures.
author: Analysts Account
